command: env GPUS_PER_NODE=4 CANARY_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 CANARY_REQUIRE_IDLE_GPUS=true CANARY_SEQ_LEN=512 CANARY_STEPS=1 CANARY_MBS=1 CANARY_GBS=64 CANARY_CHECKPOINT_ENABLE=false CANARY_DEDICATED_EP_PG=true CANARY_ACTIVATION_CHECKPOINT_MODE=none CANARY_SPARSE_MLA_MAX_SEQ_LEN=128 MASTER_PORT=29542 RUN_ID=torchtitan_direct_dsv4_s512_densefallback_4xmi350_noac_20260601T000000 experiments/2026-05-27-deepseek-v4-amd-pretrain/launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh run

target_comparison.caveat: This S512 dense-fallback isolation is a stability check for the materialized sparse-MLA guard, not final S4096 sparse-MLA performance.

mfu_note: The raw TorchTitan MFU in this run used the old A100 fallback peak of 312e12. The MI350X-normalized field uses dense BF16 peak 2309.6e12.
read: Forcing dense fallback at S512 clears the 4xMI350 backward fault, localizing the failure to the bounded PyTorch sparse-MLA/LightningIndexer path rather than generic S512 TorchTitan/FSDP/EP. Throughput is still far below the normalized 4xMI350 target.
